data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 2030 GMT on Friday:

** U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to give his approval to Nippon Steel's $14.9 billion bid for U.S. Steel on Friday, saying the "planned partnership" between the two would create jobs and help the American economy.

** Brazilian businessman Nelson Tanure has made an offer to acquire a controlling stake of petrochemical company Braskem , two sources familiar with the matter said on Friday.

** CommScope Holding is exploring the sale of its broadband connectivity and cable solutions (CCS) division, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

** Shareholders of Swiss insurer Helvetia approved a plan for the business to merge with its rival Baloise , the company said.

** Generali announced that it has completed the sale of 100% of its stake in Generali Life Assurance Philippines to Insular Life Assurance Company.

** RedBird Capital Partners, the U.S. fund that bought Britain's Telegraph newspaper in partnership with UAE-owned IMI, will take control of the publication after agreeing a deal that values the enterprise at 500 million pounds ($673 million).

** The U.S. Federal Trade Commission dropped a case that sought to block Microsoft's <MSFT.O> $69 billion purchase of "Call of Duty" maker Activision Blizzard, saying on Thursday that pursuing the case against the long-closed deal was not in the public interest.

** Lumber company Weyerhaeuser <WY.N> said on Thursday it will acquire nearly 117,000 acres of timberlands in North Carolina and Virginia from a unit of Roseburg Forest Products for $375 million.

** OnlyFans owner Fenix International Ltd is in talks to sell the porn-driven company to an investor group at a valuation of around $8 billion, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

data.title: Marin Software Receives Nasdaq Noncompliance Notice
data.description:
Marin Software said late Friday it received a notice from Nasdaq due to noncompliance with listing standards.

The company failed to file its quarterly Form 10-Q and the Form 10-K annual report on time, according to a statement.

The company said it has till June 16 to submit a plan to regain compliance.

data.title: Oyster Enterprises II Acquisition Closes $253 Million IPO
data.description:
Oyster Enterprises II Acquisition reported late Friday the closing of its initial public offering of 25.3 million units, including the full exercise of an over-allotment option, at $10 apiece.

Each company unit comprises a class A share and one right to receive one-tenth of a class A share upon closing of the business combination, the blank check company said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Trump Reportedly Approves Nippon Steel's Acquisition of US Steel; US Steel Shares Rise After Hours
data.description:
President Donald Trump has approved Nippon Steel's acquisition of United States Steel , Nikkei reported Friday, quoting unnamed White House sources.

Trump said Friday in a post on Truth Social that US Steel will remain in the US and keep its headquarters in Pittsburgh after a "planned partnership" with Japan's Nippon Steel.

The partnership between the two steelmakers will "create at least 70,000 jobs" and will add $14 billion to the US economy, Trump said in the post.

Trump said he will be at US Steel headquarters in Pittsburgh on May 30 for a rally.

US Steel didn't immediately respond to a request for comment by MT Newswires.

US Steel shares jumped past 3% in recent after-hours activity.

data.title: ETHEMA HEALTH Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ethema Health Corporation, a company that transitioned from oil and gas exploration to healthcare services in 2010, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive addiction treatment industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $6.02 million, an increase of 12.6% from the previous year, driven by organic growth in patient treatment revenue.
  • Operating loss: $1.33 million, an increase in loss of 146.0% due to higher operating expenses.
  • Net (loss) income: $(2.17) million, a significant decrease from the previous year's net income, primarily due to increased operating losses and the absence of prior year gains on property disposal.
  • Net (loss) income available to common stockholders of Ethema Health Corporation: $(2.06) million, reflecting the overall net loss for the year.
  • Basic (loss) income per common share: $(0.00), indicating no earnings per share due to the net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Ethema Health Corporation, originally engaged in oil and gas exploration, transitioned to healthcare services in 2010, focusing on addiction treatment centers in the USA and Canada.
  • Operational Shift: In 2010, Ethema shifted from electronics to healthcare services, initially consulting with Greenstone Clinic in Canada to develop medical clinics, and later obtaining a license in Florida for addiction treatment services.
  • Geographical Expansion: The company expanded its operations to Florida, USA, in 2017, acquiring Seastone Delray's business and real estate assets to enhance its addiction treatment services.
  • Facility Developments: Ethema moved its ARIA operations to West Palm Beach in 2018, receiving a license for in-patient detoxification and residential treatment services.
  • Strategic Acquisitions: In 2020, Ethema acquired a majority stake in American Treatment Holdings, Inc., which operates drug rehabilitation facilities, and later acquired the remaining interest in 2024.
  • Recent Acquisitions: In 2024, Ethema acquired assets and assumed leases for Boca Cove Detox, LLC, expanding its operational capacity in Boca Raton, Florida.
  • Lease Agreements: In 2023, Ethema entered a long-term lease for its West Palm Beach facility, ensuring operational stability with a 20-year term and options for extension.
  • Future Outlook: Ethema plans to continue seeking additional opportunities for addiction treatment in the US markets, requiring approximately $3.8 million in funding to support its operations and growth initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Acquisitions: Ethema Health Corporation has been actively involved in strategic acquisitions to expand its operations. In 2024, the company acquired the remaining 25% of ATHI, consolidating its control over the subsidiary. Additionally, the company acquired assets and assumed leases for the Boca Cove Detox Center, enhancing its service offerings in the addiction treatment sector. These acquisitions are part of the company's strategy to strengthen its market position and expand its operational footprint.
  • Capital Management: The company engaged in several capital management activities, including raising $1.9 million through short-term notes and repaying $0.8 million, resulting in a net increase of $1.1 million. It also converted related party payables into equity, issuing 4 billion common shares and 600,000 Series A preferred shares to improve its balance sheet. Furthermore, Ethema Health Corporation disposed of its real estate subsidiary, Cranberry Cove Holdings, to a related party, which helped streamline its operations and focus on core business activities.
  • Future Outlook: Ethema Health Corporation anticipates requiring approximately $3.8 million in funding over the next twelve months to meet its obligations and support working capital needs. The company plans to continue seeking additional opportunities for addiction treatment in the US markets. However, there is a high liquidity risk, and the company acknowledges the need for additional capital through equity or debt financing to sustain its operations and implement its business plan. The management is aware of the potential for dilution and other financial constraints if additional funds are raised.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: Ethema Health Corporation has undergone significant changes in its business operations, transitioning from oil and gas exploration to healthcare services, specifically addiction treatment. This shift presents operational risks as the company navigates a highly competitive and regulated industry. The company has expanded its operations through acquisitions, such as the purchase of Boca Cove Detox and Edgewater Recovery, which introduces integration risks and the challenge of obtaining necessary licenses and approvals to operate these facilities.
  • Financial Challenges: The company faces financial challenges, including operating losses and a significant working capital deficiency. The increase in operating expenses, particularly in rent and general administrative costs, has contributed to a higher operating loss. The company also faces liquidity risks, with a high need for additional funding to meet its obligations and support its business plan. Management acknowledges the substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, emphasizing the need for additional capital through equity or debt financing.
  • Market Risks: The competitive landscape of the addiction treatment industry and the need for strategic marketing to attract patients pose significant market risks. The company's reliance on in-network relationships with healthcare providers also poses a risk if these relationships are disrupted or if reimbursement rates change.

SEC Filing: ETHEMA HEALTH Corp [ GRST ] - 10-K - May. 23, 2025

data.title: Sunnova Energy Receives NYSE Noncompliance Notice

Sunnova Energy International said late Friday it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange for noncompliance with listing requirements.

data.title: Chile's ENAMI says lithium venture with Rio Tinto to start production in 2032
data.description:
Chile's state-run mining firm ENAMI aims for its new Rio Tinto lithium partnership at Altoandinos to begin production in 2032 with 35,000 metric tons of the battery metal per year, and ramp up over three years to 75,000 tons, ENAMI company chief Ivan Mlynarz said on Friday.

The Altoandinos project, as well as the Maricunga project that Rio Tinto , was tapped this week to spearhead alongside state-run copper producer Codelco, will give the global miner a critical role in Chile's lithium industry alongside long-established players SQM and Albemarle .

Rio Tinto will initially put forward $425 million to the project, which ENAMI said will require a total investment of $3 billion.

ENAMI previously estimated the Altoandinos project capacity to be 60,000 tons a year, before new studies showed more resources than expected, particularly at the La Isla salt flat.

Mlynarz said ENAMI plans for the project to start with direct lithium extraction (DLE), an innovative method that has yet to be used in Chile, and that Rio Tinto is testing at its Rincon project in Argentina.

ENAMI has begun testing DLE options from various companies, and Mlynarz said early results from Rio Tinto's technology look promising, paving the way for its potential use on the project.

"The results have been encouraging with Rio Tinto, and it has the advantage of having the operator use their own technology," Mlynarz said.

He added that the partnership needed approval from international regulatory agencies, but that ENAMI in the meantime would continue exploration studies, with the hope that Rio Tinto will take the lead in 2026.

"We need to keep working in the salt flat because both ENAMI and Rio Tinto know that timing is key," Mlynarz said.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1440 ET [Dow Jones]--After releasing updates across its portfolio in the last few months, Wix.com's product offerings appear increasingly likely to improve customer conversion, retention and monetization over time, Baird analysts said in a research note. In addition to the latest features, including an AI visual design platform and an AI assistant, the company seems to have more on the horizon for later this year and beyond, the analysts said. Such drivers, as well as strong first-quarter results, add more credibility to the company's full-year outlook, which guides for sales growth of 12% to 14%, the analysts said. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1427 ET - Apple has sought to avoid the brunt of tariff pressures by shipping iPhone products to the US from India and Vietnam, but it hasn't been enough to placate President Trump. Trump said today iPhones manufactured abroad could face import taxes of at least 25%, noting he expects the devices to be made in the U.S. and not in China, India, or elsewhere. "Given the deal with China, Apple will have been hoping that tensions with the US government would begin to ease, but this has appeared to be a false dawn," says Quilter Cheviot's Ben Barringer. "Given the renewed rhetoric coming from Trump, this position may need to change, and greater consideration given to either upping prices, moving supply chains or ultimately both." (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1351 ET - Despite notching continued sales growth above expectations in its latest quarter, Workday investors appear unimpressed with the company's reaffirmed guidance as it tumbles 11%. The move "may be evidence of a material swing in investor expectations into the April quarter prints," UBS analysts say in a research note. Just a month ago, reaffirmed guidance could have driven a relief rally, they say, as investors feared a slowdown in corporate spending amid macroeconomic and tariff-related headwinds. Other software companies, like Palantir and Monday.com, have bumped up their full-year expectations in recent weeks after posting continued quarterly sales growth, relieving investors, and potentially raising the bar for similar companies.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1327 ET - With a $795 million modification, Palantir's US Army contract for its Maven Smart System is now worth up to about $1.3 billion, marking the company's first agreement to cross the billion-dollar threshold, Wedbush Securities analysts say in a research note. The deal adds further momentum for Palantir's federal business in North America, which will likely increase from President Trump's plans for an air and missile defense shield, the analysts say. With a NATO partnership also helping it gain traction in Europe, Palantir is in a sweet spot to benefit from governments in both continents going all-in on efficiency and AI aims, and will likely gain more federal deals going forward, the analysts say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1304 ET - Workday's unchanged outlook after logging higher than expected revenue last quarter, seems to be a conservatism move in the face of economic uncertainty, Bank of America Securities analysts say in a research note. "It is clear though that macro pressure is still unseen, suggesting potential for upside if the environment holds," the analysts say, suggesting the setup could help the company beat current guidance. Workday logged 13% subscription sales growth last quarter, beating expectations from both analysts and the company itself. Workday slides 11%. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1101 ET - When President Trump backed down from his harsh bilateral "Liberation Day" tariffs last month, investors took heart that the "Trump put" was alive and well--a bet that Trump's instincts for appeasement in the face of market downturns place a floor under asset prices. Brent Donnelly of Spectra Markets says new tariff threats show another feature of Trump's reaction function: When markets stabilize, Trump takes it as a blessing to resume his previous provocations. It looks more like a "Trump strangle," a reference to an options strategy that bets on volatility, agnostic to direction. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1058 ET - Canada's uranium producers jump of hopes that President Trump will sign an executive order that could kick off more demand for nuclear power. Energy Fuels, NexGen Energy, Denison Mines and Cameco are among the biggest winners, rising 17%, 12%, 11% and 8%, respectively. Nuclear energy is seen as a popular choice to keep up with heavy demands for data center power requirements. Trump is expected to streamline the process which could give a jolt to the industry. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1028 ET - Intuit sees a few reasons to think it has pricing power. CEO Sasan Goodarzi on an earnings call notes that the company known for products including TurboTax and Credit Karma is mission-critical for many businesses, adding that more than 90% of Intuit is recurring revenue and subscription-based. He also believes the company is a low-cost disruptor in the assisted tax segment, particularly when compared to businesses spending on a number of services that don't communicate with each other. "Therefore, we have a lot of ARPC [average revenue per customer] tailwinds as we look ahead," Goodarzi says. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1019 ET - Intuit, the company known for products including TurboTax and Credit Karma says data and AI powered many elements of its customer experience this tax season. Its done-for-you experience drove a 12% reduction in average time a customer spent on their return, with more than half of its do-it-yourself and do-it-with-me customers completing their return in under one hour, says CEO Sasan Goodarzi on an earnings call. Intuit has invested more in AI in recent months, with its efforts paying off in the latest quarter as its tax business saw strong results in F3Q, including an acceleration in revenue growth in TurboTax Live, where AI agents and experts support customers. Intuit surges 9% on better-than-expected F4Q guidance a raised outlook for the year. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0942 ET - President Trump's new tariff declaration "is a destabilizing threat that could do real, lasting damage," deVere Group's Nigel Green says in a note. "Beyond the short-term volatility, there's a bigger risk unfolding: the risk of recession and erosion of US credibility on the global stage," he says. The dollar and Treasury yields went up a little after Trump's social media post threatening 50% tariff on the E.U. and 25% on imported iPhones, but remained below yesterday's levels. The 10-year yield is at 4.483% and the WSJ Dollar Index falls 0.5%. The greenback weakens 0.8% versus the yen and 0.3% against the euro. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0847 ET - Apple producing iPhones in the U.S. is "a fairy tale that is not feasible," says Wedbush's Dan Ives in a research note. President Trump lobbied fresh criticism on Apple on his social media platform and called for a 25% tariff if the company doesn't manufacture and build its product in the U.S. Apple has moved iPhone production to India and away from China in recent years. The analyst notes that iPhones produced in the U.S. would put its prices around $3,500, a non-starter for the company and consumers alike. Apple sinks 3.2% premarket. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0840 ET - Treasury yields and the dollar fall as Trump threatens a 50% tariff on the E.U. starting June 1 and a 25% tariff on imported iPhones. Yields and the dollar were going down overnight and briefly changed direction after the tweets, while remaining below yesterday levels. In a nod to the fiscal concerns that rocked markets this week, Deutsche Bank's George Saravelos writes that the challenge around Trump's trade policies is that the U.S. deficit "requires ongoing funding from foreigners to be sustained." There is no major data point on tap ahead of Memorial Day. The 10-year yield is at 4.485% and the two-year at 3.929%. The WSJ Dollar Index falls 0.6%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1308 ET - Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says in a TV interview with Bloomberg that a regulatory reform aimed at improving conditions in the Treasury market could make progress soon. The reform would shift the supplementary leverage ratio to decrease the capital that banks must hold against portfolios of Treasury securities, making it less expensive for them to own U.S. government debt. "That is moving along very quickly between the three banking regulators, the Fed, the OCC and the FDIC, so I would think we could see something on that over the summer," Bessent says. He cites estimates that the reform could lower Treasury yields by tenths of a percentage point. "Banks are being penalized for holding Treasurys," Bessent says of the current regulatory regime. Some analysts counter that the reform wouldn't have a meaningful impact on the Treasury market. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0927 ET - Company earnings in Canada rose in the first quarter, driven by financial industries and with higher energy prices. Statistics Canada calculates pretax income across corporates rose 2.7% to reach C$161.2 billion collectively. Financial sector earnings were up 7.2%, with increases for banks and insurers. Earnings for non-financial sectors were up 0.9%, the agency says. with oil and gas extraction notching the strongest advance thanks in part to a rebound in crude oil prices and increased export activity. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0821 ET - European bank shares are among the biggest declines amid widespread losses in the region's stocks after U.S. President Trump threatened new tariffs on imports from the European Union. The Stoxx Europe 600 Banks index is down 3.8%, while the Euro Stoxx Banks index of eurozone banks falls 4.4%. Italy's Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, France's Societe Generale and Spain's CaixaBank are among the biggest losers, all down more than 5%. Deutsche Bank, Banco Santander, BNP Paribas and UniCredit all shed more than 4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0638 ET - The high level of transparency and precision provided by Generali in its first-quarter results is appreciated, Berenberg says in a research note. Management explained the gap between its operating profit result and consensus expectations, what drove the better-than-expected undiscounted combined ratio and what will drive its life operating profit in the year, analyst Michael Huttner points out. "We believe this level of visibility merits a higher valuation multiple, and for this reason we raise our price target modestly from 34.8 euros to 36.1 euros," he notes. Shares in the Italian insurer are up 20% since the start of the year and trade at 32.8 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0513 ET - ABN Amro has scope to improve its capital consumption after years of not managing it as efficiently as it could have, UBS says in a note. The Dutch lender is set to outline an updated strategy in November, with focuses on profitability as well as cost and capital optimization. Actions such as significant risk transfers, improving data and selected exits over the next five years could add close to 2 billion euros in excess capital, analysts Johan Ekblom and Michael Macnaughton calculate. These extra resources could then increase potential buybacks while maintaining a healthy capital buffer and lead to a lift in return on equity, they note. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0424 ET - UniCredit is proving that it will push forward with its offer for Banco BPM despite the challenges, Equita says in a research note. Italy's securities regulator recently imposed 30-day suspension on the bid, to which UniCredit responded by saying it remains in talks with the government to iron out the conditions it is imposing on the deal. "The press release provides further transparency on UCG's initiatives and highlights, in our view, the willingness to go ahead with the offer," analyst Andrea Lisi writes. The final decision will depend on the outcome of the talks between UniCredit and the government and conditions set out by Rome to clear the transaction. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0406 ET - Credit Agricole's return to Belgium could eventually ramp up competition in the country's banking sector, ING says in a research note. The French bank bought a 9.9% stake in Crelan, the Belgian lender it used to own half of, as part of a long-term partnership between the groups which both have roots in agricultural lending. "While it is early days, having a second French bank after BNP Paribas coming back to expand in Belgium could create at some point more sizeable competition, or a strong 5th larger bank," analyst Jason Kalamboussis writes. He notes that this time Credit Agricole has a stronger foothold in Belgium given its recent acquisition of wealth manager Degroof Petercam last year, which it is focused on integrating. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0153 ET - Bank Jago's challenges are likely to persist for the rest of 2025, Nomura's Tushar Mohata says in a research report. These include asset quality and a likely decline in savings rates in Indonesia's mass-market segments recently, the analyst says. Also, a gradual reduction in the lending-rate cap for fintechs, which are lending partners of digital banks, could affect Bank Jago's overall earnings. Moreover, loan growth for digital banks could slow, says Nomura, which cuts its 2025-2026 loan-growth assumptions for the digital bank by 3%-15%. The brokerage lowers the stock's target price to IDR2,275.00 from IDR3,000.00, while maintaining a neutral rating. A Verdhana Sekuritas analyst contributed to the report. Shares are 0.5% lower at IDR2,010.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0026 ET - Bendigo and Adelaide Bank looks to be on track to meet consensus expectations for its full-year cash profit, but UBS analyst John Storey still has a wary eye on the lender's pre-provision performance. Storey tells clients in a note that Bendigo now needs A$120 million in 4Q cash profit to hit full-year expectations, which he thinks is achievable. However, he warns that Bendigo has work to do if its pre-provision profit is to meet the average analyst forecast. He points out that 3Q pre-provision profit fell 4% on the average of the prior two quarters, and needs to grow by 4.8% in 4Q to meet expectations. UBS has a neutral rating and A$11.00 target price on the stock, which is flat at A$11.77. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0008 ET - Bendigo & Adelaide Bank's third-quarter update shows margin discipline in the face of lower interest rates and increased competition, Jarden analysts say. They think that the regional Australian lender did well to hold its net interest margin flat on the prior three months, but see compression resuming over coming periods against the backdrop of central-bank rate cuts. Bendigo's headline profit was about 2% stronger than the Jarden analysts had anticipated, which they tell clients in a note was due to benign credit quality. Jarden raises its target price 0.9% to A$11.60 and stays neutral on the stock, which is flat at A$11.77. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2114 ET - Srisawad Corp.'s earnings recovery has likely been delayed, Thanachart Securities' Sarachada Sornsong says in a research report. Its 1Q profits were weighed by worsening asset quality in new motorcycle hire-purchase loans, with its subsidiary Srisawad Capital posting a 6% on quarter contraction in this segment, the analyst notes. The Thailand financial services provider's title loan growth has trailed behind peers, with Srisawad attributing this to its more cautious lending stance amid heightened economic uncertainty. The brokerage cuts its 2025-2027 earnings estimates for Srisawad by average 17%. It lowers the stock's target price to THB25.00 from THB37.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares last closed at THB18.70. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1520 ET - Oil futures end a choppy trading week with modest declines as the market frets about the June 1 OPEC+ meeting and prospects of the group adding another 411,000 barrels a day to production in July. "A drop lower in prices could be expected over the next week but we are unlikely to test our previous lows," Alex Hodes of StoneX says in a note. Trade issues resurface with President Trump's threat of a 50% tariff on the EU, while the U.S. and Iran ended nuclear talks in Rome without a deal, but agreeing to continue negotiating. WTI and Brent settle up 0.5% at $61.53 and $64.78 a barrel, respectively, for weekly losses of 1.5% and 1%. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1339 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by eight this week to 465, oil services company Baker Hughes reports, reaching its lowest level since November 2021 as producers react to low oil prices. "The falling price of oil has surely contributed to curb investment in U.S. shale during the course of the past few months. But another factor that has come to bite companies is the rising wellhead oil breakevens," Francisco Blanch of Bank of America Securities says in a note. And with shale productivity gains slowing, "total U.S. crude oil supply could peak as soon as the next 24 months," he adds. Rigs directed at natural gas fell by two this week to 98, down by 1 from a year ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1253 ET - Investments in sectors backed by real assets--areas such as real estate, infrastructure, utilities and energy--are likely to show more stability than the broader equity market amid policy-driven volatility, Cohen & Steers multi-asset head Jeff Palma tells WSJ. He adds the caveat that some classes of real assets, in particular real estate and infrastructure, remain sensitive to swings in interest rates. But the real-asset space nonetheless gives investors attractive diversification, Palma says. Year to date, the S&P utilities index has gained 5.9% and the S&P real-estate index is down 0.8%, compared with a 1.4% decline year to date in the broader S&P 500. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1204 ET - Oil futures shake off early weakness and turn higher, while remaining on track for a small weekly loss driven by concerns that OPEC+ will decide on another large output increase for July. A renewal of trade tensions as President Trump says he will place a 50% tariff on the EU on June 1 injects volatility into the market, and traders are watching for the outcome of talks on Iran's nuclear program. With Iran insisting on enriching uranium for energy production, "an agreement still seems farfetched at this point," Alex Hodes of StoneX says in a note. Lack of agreement is seen keeping U.S. sanctions on Iranian oil in place, while an accord could add Iranian barrels to the market. WTI is up 0.8%, at $61.67 a barrel, and Brent is up 0.7%, at $64.87.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1105 ET - ING Economics says in a note that "it has always been clear that the U.S. administration's stance on the EU will be different than with most other trading partners." Trump's Truth Social post calling for a new 50% tariff on EU shipments has given safe haven futures like gold a boost, while other agricultural futures are mostly underwater. Traders are nervous that the new spat will escalate, although many investors are unconvinced that Trump will follow through with threats, or at least won't for an extended period of time. "Anything is possible, from a potential agreement to further escalation," says ING. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0927 ET - Company earnings in Canada rose in the first quarter, driven by financial industries and with higher energy prices. Statistics Canada calculates pretax income across corporates rose 2.7% to reach C$161.2 billion collectively. Financial sector earnings were up 7.2%, with increases for banks and insurers. Earnings for non-financial sectors were up 0.9%, the agency says. with oil and gas extraction notching the strongest advance thanks in part to a rebound in crude oil prices and increased export activity. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0910 ET - Oil futures are lower for a fourth consecutive session with trade concerns back in the mix as President Trump threatens the EU with a 50% tariff starting June 1. That's the same day that OPEC+ is due to meet to discuss July output levels amid expectations the group will increase its targets by another 411,000 barrels a day. "The combination of fiscal uncertainty and potential OPEC+ production increases is currently capping oil's recovery prospects," Pepperstone strategist Quasar Elizundia says in a note. WTI is down 1.1% at $60.51 a barrel and Brent is down 1.1% at $63.73. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0602 ET - Enel's shareholders approving a share buyback is positive news which backs indications from the company in recent months that it intends to support shareholder returns, Equita Sim analyst Roberto Letizia says in a research note. "We believe that the approval of the shareholders' meeting will allow management to start a first tranche of buybacks in the coming months," the analyst says. Shareholders approved late Thursday a share buyback for a maximum of 500 million shares at a maximum disbursement of 3.5 billion euros. The authorization is granted for a period of 18 months. Shares in the Rome-based energy company trade down 0.1% at 8.12 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

2335 ET - Bumi Armada's balance sheet will likely remain supported by strong operating cash flows and ongoing deleveraging, which has reduced net debt for 20 consecutive quarters, Maybank IB analyst Jeremie Yap says in a note. A new lease-and-operate floating production, storage and offloading contract would be a key re-rating catalyst to replenish its orderbook and improve earnings visibility, he adds. Yap cuts Bumi Armada's 2025-2027 net profit estimates by 12%, 12%, and 13%, respectively, to factor in lower FPSO Kraken profit margins. The asset's day charter rate will be reduced by 70% from 2Q, as it enters its optional extension period. Maybank cuts Bumi Armada's target price to MYR0.73 from MYR0.78, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR0.51. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1923 ET - The delay in ramping up Fortescue's Iron Bridge project to nameplate capacity disappoints Macquarie analysts, who expect little cost relief from the miner pushing out its production target. "FMG carries a large fixed-cost base at Iron Bridge," the analysts say in a note. "Therefore, we assume the volume cuts flow directly to the bottom line." However, they take a positive view on executive changes, which appear to indicate a refreshed focus on the metals business, they say. "One of our criticisms on FMG's 'FFI' strategy has been it's non-adjacency and limited grounds for genuine competitive advantage," say the analysts. "This move further aligns decarbonization and mining, potentially driving efficiency in operational decarbonization and green steel efforts." Macquarie has a "neutral" rating and A$15.00 target on Fortescue, which ended Thursday at A$15.89. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1413 ET - Gold, copper, and silver rose after President Trump threatened a 50% tariff on EU imports starting June 1. For gold, traders think strength will stay consistent into the fall and winter months. "We expect some strong range trading opportunities between $3,100 a troy ounce and $3,500 a troy ounce," says Maximilian Layton of Citi Research in a note. Front-month gold closed up 2.2% for the day, to $3,363.60 a troy ounce. Gold rose 5.7% for the week. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1105 ET - ING Economics says in a note that "it has always been clear that the U.S. administration's stance on the EU will be different than with most other trading partners." Trump's Truth Social post calling for a new 50% tariff on EU shipments has given safe haven futures like gold a boost, while other agricultural futures are mostly underwater. Traders are nervous that the new spat will escalate, although many investors are unconvinced that Trump will follow through with threats, or at least won't for an extended period of time. "Anything is possible, from a potential agreement to further escalation," says ING. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1058 ET - Canada's uranium producers jump of hopes that President Trump will sign an executive order that could kick off more demand for nuclear power. Energy Fuels, NexGen Energy, Denison Mines and Cameco are among the biggest winners, rising 17%, 12%, 11% and 8%, respectively. Nuclear energy is seen as a popular choice to keep up with heavy demands for data center power requirements. Trump is expected to streamline the process which could give a jolt to the industry. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0352 ET - Gold is on track to post a weekly gain of more than 3%, supported by a softer U.S. dollar and concerns over the country' fiscal and economic outlook. In early trade, futures rise 1.1% to $3,331 a troy ounce, while the U.S. dollar index slips 0.5% to 99.45. "Gold is showing strength again, as it is particularly in demand in these politically uncertain times, also because other supposed safe havens such as the U.S. dollar are currently under pressure," says Barbara Lambrecht, commodity analyst at Commerzbank Research. Focus this week has shifted to the U.S. fiscal outlook after the House passed President Trump's tax-and-spending bill, which analysts say could significantly inflate the government deficit in the coming years. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

2231 ET - The market will likely expect the successor for Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm to be an internal candidate, according to Citi analyst Paul McTaggart. He views iron-ore chief Simon Trott and Chief Commercial Officer Bold Baatar, a former copper boss, as the likely front runners. Like other analysts, McTaggart says the succession plan came as a surprise. "We won't speculate as to why Stausholm is leaving earlier than most would have expected," he says in a note. Citi has a neutral rating and A$130.00 target on Rio Tinto, which is down 1.0% at A$117.64.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2200 ET - Press Metal Aluminium's long-term outlook may remain supported by tight aluminum supply despite headwinds from U.S. tariffs, say Maybank IB analysts Loh Yan Jin and Jeremie Yap in a note. The company's upstream and downstream expansions are expected to support margin stability and mitigate supply chain risks, they say. However, the analysts cut Press Metal's 2025-2027 net profit forecasts by 3%, 5%, and 5%, respectively, to reflect lower aluminum price assumptions and reduced associate contributions. Maybank lowers Press Metal's target price to MYR5.64 from MYR5.82 after 1Q earnings came in slightly below expectations, but maintains a buy rating on favorable supply dynamics. Shares are up 0.4% at MYR5.05. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1938 ET - Morgans views recent setbacks at EQ Resources' Mount Carbine tungsten mine in Queensland state as a blip. Production fell by more than 50% in the three months through December due to the availability of process water, according to analyst Chris Brown. Then, seasonal wet weather stymied efforts to bring in consumables, including explosives, from Townsville, and production fell further. "Our expectation is for a return to normal operating conditions for the balance of 2025," says Morgans. "We expect a major turn-around for Mt Carbine, returning to previous mining rates, and benefiting from an increased head grade." Morgans, which rates EQ Resources as a "speculative buy," expects the operation will be cash flow positive. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1923 ET - The delay in ramping up Fortescue's Iron Bridge project to nameplate capacity disappoints Macquarie analysts, who expect little cost relief from the miner pushing out its production target. "FMG carries a large fixed-cost base at Iron Bridge," the analysts say in a note. "Therefore, we assume the volume cuts flow directly to the bottom line." However, they take a positive view on executive changes, which appear to indicate a refreshed focus on the metals business, they say. "One of our criticisms on FMG's 'FFI' strategy has been it's non-adjacency and limited grounds for genuine competitive advantage," say the analysts. "This move further aligns decarbonization and mining, potentially driving efficiency in operational decarbonization and green steel efforts." Macquarie has a "neutral" rating and A$15.00 target on Fortescue, which ended Thursday at A$15.89. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1031 ET - The pick up in Canadian consumer spending in the latter half of 2024 has cooled and is likely to continue slowing this year as economic uncertainty remains high, tariffs weigh on activity and the labor market weakens further, argues Citi's Veronica Clark. Retail sales in Canada rebounded in March with a 0.9% rise after declines in January and February, and rose just 0.8% on an annualized basis for 1Q. Clark says some tariff front-running may have played into March's numbers, with auto sales up strongly following a weak start to the year but retail trade in others goods categories have been mixed. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1025 ET - The Canadian retail-sales report for March was decent, if you look through the tariff- and energy-price-driven swings, BMO Economics' Shelly Kaushik says. Sales rose 0.8% on month, a tick stronger than the prior flash estimate and with a big boost from auto sales ahead of tariffs. Spending in volume terms was up 0.9% for the month, pushing the quarterly annualized growth rate to 0.8%. Kaushik says that although 1Q real GDP data only comes out next week, for now it looks set to come in line with Statistics Canada's advance estimate for 1.5% annualized growth. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1024 ET — Canadian consumers apparently haven't tightened the purse strings much more in the face of tariff uncertainty, and retail spending appears to have been a little firmer in March and April than it was at the start of 1Q when worse-than-usual winter weather may have held back consumption, says CIBC Capital Markets' Andrew Grantham. Overall retail sales volumes rose by 0.9% in March, and were 3.3% higher on-year, though much of that was been driven by auto sales. Still, Grantham says that even with consumer spending holding up better than anticipated he expects a modest contraction in GDP during 2Q, driven by a weakness in manufacturing and other export-sensitive sectors. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0954 ET - Statistics Canada's flash estimate for April retail sales indicates receipts rose 0.5%, though Desjardins' Royce Mendes says that given a rise in goods prices during the month volumes will likely look materially more muted. The economist notes tariff-related increases in prices showed up in April's inflation release and likely meant Canadians were paying more for a host of goods included in the retail sales metrics. The latest retail sales data don't change Desjardins' view the Bank of Canada will cut rates another quarter percentage point in June, though next week's GDP numbers remain a hurdle. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0952 ET - The latest Canadian retail data backs signs people were rushing to buy a vehicle before the Trump administration's auto import tariffs and Canada's retaliatory measures kicked in last month. Canadian retail sales rose 0.8% on-month in March, a tick stronger than the 0.7% rise economists expected and following declines the first two months of the year. The advance was driven by motor vehicle and parts dealers, with sales climbing for the first time in three months and notching the strongest advance since January 2023. March retail sales excluding vehicles and parts fell 0.7%. Earlier Statistics Canada data showed March sales of new vehicles climbed 9.4% on-year, thanks mainly to outlays on new light trucks. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0929 ET - A jump in Canadian motor vehicle sales in March suggests tariff front-running has supported consumption, though the big picture for the month is retail were strong in most key sector, Capital Economics' Stephen Brown says. Retail sales rose 0.8% on-month after February's 0.5% drop. Brown new vehicle sales were up 5.2%, matching trends in the U.S. The economist adds an advance estimate for a 0.5% rise in April retail sales is more positive than expected, and may imply a small rise in sales volumes and lends support to his forecast that GDP growth will slow sharply rather than contract this quarter. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0820 ET - Shares of automotive groups in the European Union are falling after President Trump threatened a 50% tariff on the bloc. The auto industry already faces a 25% tariff for imports to the U.S. "Our discussions with them are going nowhere! Therefore, I am recommending a straight 50% Tariff on the European Union, starting on June 1, 2025," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social. Shares of Jeep maker Stellantis are down 4.8% in Milan. In Germany, Volkswagen shares are down 3.1%, while Mercedes-Benz Group stock is down 4.3%. In Italy, Ferrari shares are down 3.8%. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0740 ET - For European car-parts makers, tire markets were resilient and profitable in the first quarter, despite significant macroeconomic and tariff volatility, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. A key swing factor from here for earnings will be specialty tires, with Michelin being largely exposed, the analysts say. The analysts upgrade Michelin to a buy recommendation given the stronger earnings outlook, as well as relative valuations and flexibility from a conservative balance sheet. Continental is downgraded to hold and Pirelli remains at buy, they say. Michelin and Pirelli shares trade 1.1% and 0.6% higher, respectively. Continental shares trade 0.7% lower. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0537 ET - Kuehne + Nagel's acquisition of Transporte y Distribucion Nacional is complementary to the Swiss logistics group's existing network and will lift earnings, Vontobel analysts say in a research note. "We estimate a deal value in the lower double-digit million range, and no material impact on our valuation and investment case," the analysts say. Kuehne + Nagel's purchase will expand its European network, especially in Iberia. The company didn't disclose financial details regarding its agreement to acquire the Spanish company. Kuehne + Nagel's shares trade 0.05% down to 192 Swiss francs. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0413 ET - Italian aerospace and defense group Leonardo remains in pole position to acquire Iveco's military vehicles business due to political and industrial reasons, Equita analyst Martino De Ambroggi says. He cites media reports that suggest that Czech industrial and technology group CSG has also submitted a non-binding offer for the asset that could far exceed the proposal made jointly by Leonardo and Rheinmetall for 1.5 billion euros. Spain's Indra Sistemas, which has also submitted a bid, could withdraw it or submit it jointly with Leonardo in order to take part in the Leonardo-Rheinmetall joint venture on tanks and armored vehicles. Dow Jones Newswires reported Thursday that Indra expressed interest in participating in the joint venture in some form. Shares in Leonardo are flat, Indra is up 0.5% and Rheinmetall is down 0.4%. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0046 ET - HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering's 2H earnings are likely to improve on higher-end contract wins, Kiwoom Securities analyst Lee Han-gyeol writes in a note. Lee expects the South Korean shipbuilder to secure strong orders from clients for LNG carriers in 2H, thanks to the U.S. plan to resume LNG exports. He forecasts the company to meet its 2025 new-contract target of $16.2B without difficulty. South Korean shipyards are also enjoying a 5%-10% premium to global contract prices in the wake of new U.S. restrictions on Chinese shipbuilders, he adds. Kiwoom raises the stock's target by 20% to KRW347,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 0.2% lower at KRW287,500. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

2335 ET - Bumi Armada's balance sheet will likely remain supported by strong operating cash flows and ongoing deleveraging, which has reduced net debt for 20 consecutive quarters, Maybank IB analyst Jeremie Yap says in a note. A new lease-and-operate floating production, storage and offloading contract would be a key re-rating catalyst to replenish its orderbook and improve earnings visibility, he adds. Yap cuts Bumi Armada's 2025-2027 net profit estimates by 12%, 12%, and 13%, respectively, to factor in lower FPSO Kraken profit margins. The asset's day charter rate will be reduced by 70% from 2Q, as it enters its optional extension period. Maybank cuts Bumi Armada's target price to MYR0.73 from MYR0.78, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR0.51. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Blockware Solutions CEO Mason Jappa Highlights Strategic Growth and Innovation at Benchmark’s Digital Assets Virtual Seminar
data.description:
CHICAGO, IL / ACCESS Newswire / May 23, 2025 / Mason Jappa, CEO and Co-Founder of Blockware Solutions LLC, participated in Benchmark's Digital Assets Virtual Seminar, offering insights into the evolving landscape of Bitcoin mining and digital asset infrastructure. Drawing from his extensive experience in the industry, Jappa highlighted the critical role of transparency, accessibility, and innovation in shaping the future of cryptocurrency mining.

In his presentation, Jappa emphasized the importance of building trust within the mining community. He discussed how Blockware Solutions has been at the forefront of providing reliable hardware procurement, hosting services, and mining pool operations, ensuring that both newcomers and seasoned miners have access to the resources they need.

"Our mission at Blockware has always been to democratize access to Bitcoin mining," said Jappa. "By offering comprehensive solutions and fostering a transparent ecosystem, we aim to empower individuals and institutions to participate confidently in the digital asset space."

Jappa also addressed the challenges and opportunities presented by the current regulatory environment. He underscored the necessity for miners to adapt to changing policies while advocating for clear guidelines that support innovation and growth in the sector.

The seminar provided a platform for industry leaders to exchange ideas on the future of digital assets. Jappa's insights contributed to a broader understanding of how infrastructure development and strategic partnerships can drive the adoption of cryptocurrencies.

Quick Facts About Blockware Solutions:

Marketplace Activity: Blockware's ASIC Marketplace has enabled its users to purchase and sell live, hosted miners with ease since 2017.

Business Operations: Operating across six U.S.-based facilities in states like Kentucky, Texas, and Georgia, Blockware offers comprehensive services including Mining as a Service (MaaS), hardware procurement, hosting, and mining pool operations.

Recent Partnerships:

  • Crypto Blockchain Industries (CBI): Entered a $10 million strategic partnership to enhance Bitcoin mining efficiency and scalability, with Blockware deploying and managing next-generation Bitmain ASIC miners on behalf of CBI.

  • Soluna Holdings: Signed a two-year hosting agreement to deploy approximately 1,500 S21+ mining units at Soluna's Project Dorothy 2 data center in Texas, emphasizing sustainable Bitcoin infrastructure.

Expansion into HPC and AI: Blockware has recently announced its strategic expansion into High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) sectors, offering high-performance GPU servers and AI-optimized hardware. A new HPC marketplace is in development to facilitate access to these resources.

About the Company

BLOCKWARE SOLUTIONS, LLC is a leading Bitcoin mining infrastructure and services provider, evolving from a mining hardware brokerage to a comprehensive Mining-as-a-Service platform. With billions in hardware transactions and a trusted mining community, Blockware offers U.S.-based infrastructure that delivers premier hosting solutions, liquidity, and operational tools to optimize transparency, efficiency, and profitability. Their innovative Bitcoin-native Marketplace is transforming the way miners buy and sell hardware, hashrate, and energy contracts, providing just-in-time liquidity and driving the future of decentralized, transparent, and efficient mining. To learn more, visit www.blockwaresolutions.com

Watch the Benchmark's Digital Assets Virtual Seminar Here

Media Contact:

Nicholas Dorion

Director of High Performance Computing and Communications

Phone: +1 (917) 725-7118

Nicholas@blockwaresolutions.com

SOURCE: Blockware Solutions

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Soy processor basis bids mostly steady-firm as futures retreat
data.description:
Spot soybean basis bids were mostly steady to firm at U.S. Midwest crush plants on Friday and unchanged at elevators, with the market underpinned by slow farmer sales and weaker futures prices, merchants said.

  • The spot soy basis bid was up 2 cents a bushel on Friday at a large soy processor in Decatur, Illinois, and steady at a crush plant in Lafayette, Indiana. Spot basis bids at both locations gained 10 cents this week.

  • Spot corn basis bids were mostly steady to firm at ethanol plants and flat to weaker at other processors. Bids for rail-delivered corn were mixed.

  • Farmer sales were slow on Friday amid weaker Chicago Board of Trade futures prices. Growers in the central and northern Midwest are also turning their focus back to planting the last of their spring crops following rain delays earlier this week.

  • Farms in the Ohio River valley and the Delta are poised to receive more rain this weekend, which is likely to further delay planting progress there, according to forecasters.

  • CBOT July corn futures (CN25) settled 3-1/2 cents lower on Friday at $4.59-1/2 a bushel, while July soybeans (SN25) ended 7-1/4 cents lower at $10.60-1/4 per bushel. The market will be closed on Monday for the U.S. Memorial Day holiday.

data.title: US Equity Indexes Fall in Week on Treasury Market Jitters, Trump Warnings to EU, Apple
data.description:
US equity indexes fell this week as the White House's budget bill triggered jitters in the Treasury market, while President Donald Trump threatened to impose hefty tariffs on Apple and the EU.

* The S&P 500 fell to 5,802.82 on Friday from 5,958.38 a week earlier, the Nasdaq Composite dropped to 18,737.21 from 19,211.1, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined to 41,603.07 from 42,654.74.

* The House of Representatives voted 215-214 to approve a bill that will likely expand the deficit by $2.3 trillion over 10 years, the Washington Post reported, citing data compiled by the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office.

* "Overall, it amounts to more debt, bigger structural deficits, unwise tax policy distortions and even at that, it's not including up to half a trillion for Trump's 'Golden Dome,' which means that the true deficit and debt projections would be considerably higher over time," said Derek Holt, Scotiabank's head of capital market economics. "There is nothing substantive on infrastructure. It's all about funding tax cut extensions."

* The 30-year Treasury yield slid to 5.04% late Friday, still topping the psychologically important 5% level.

* The ICE US Dollar Index fell 0.8% to 99.12, trading close to the lowest since April 2022.

* In a social media post, Trump said trade talks with the EU are going nowhere, and he is "recommending a straight 50% tariff" on the bloc starting June 1.

* Trump threatened to impose at least a 25% tariff on Apple if the company sticks to its policy of manufacturing iPhones outside the US. The tech giant's shares dropped 3% on Friday.

data.title: Southern California Edison to pay $82.5 million to settle claims over 2020 Bobcat Fire damages
data.description:
Southern California Edison (SCE), a unit of utility Edison International , agreed on Friday to pay $82.5 million to settle claims with the U.S. Forest Service for costs and damages resulting from the Bobcat Fire in 2020.

The U.S. government had filed a lawsuit against SCE in 2023, alleging negligence that caused the wildfire, which burned nearly 180 square miles (466.2 square kilometers) in one of the largest wildfires in Los Angeles County.

In a complaint filed in Los Angeles federal court, the government alleged that the wildfire began on September 6, 2020, when a poorly maintained tree contacted power lines, igniting vegetation on a branch, which then fell to the ground and spread the fire.

"This record settlement against Southern California Edison provides meaningful compensation to taxpayers for the extensive costs of fighting the Bobcat Fire and for the widespread damage to public lands," said United States Attorney Bill Essayli.

According to U.S. Department of Justice, this is the largest-ever wildfire cost recovery settlement in the Central District of California.

The company has agreed to pay the settlement within 60 days of the effective date of the settlement agreement, which was May 14, without admitting wrongdoing or fault, the U.S. attorney's office in Los Angeles said.

"We are pleased to have resolved this matter, and will continue to advance wildfire mitigation measures to further enhance public safety and resiliency," said SCE spokesperson Diane Castro.

The company submitted a three-year wildfire mitigation plan to California's Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety last week.

data.title: DocuSign Enters Into $1B Credit Pact
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

DocuSign has entered into five-year credit pact for as much as $1 billion with Bank of America.

The agreement is for a $750 million revolving credit facility, which may be increased by an additional $250 million, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

The pact is set to mature on May 21, 2030, the e-signature company said.

As of May 21, the company had no outstanding revolving loans under the agreement, according to the filing.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Nukkleus Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nukkleus Inc., a company previously engaged in general support services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant changes in the company's business focus and provides detailed financial performance metrics for the period ending March 31, 2025. The company has undergone a strategic shift towards the defense sector, marking a pivotal change in its operational landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $0 million. The company did not report any revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2025, as it ceased its general support services operations.
  • Total Gross Profit (Loss): $(1,507,107). The company reported a gross loss due to the absence of revenue and ongoing operating expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $(1,507,107). Operating expenses were primarily driven by professional fees and general administrative costs.
  • Net Income (Loss): $102,958,139. The company achieved a significant net income primarily due to a change in fair value of stock purchase warrant liabilities.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share, Basic: $20.76. Reflects the substantial net income achieved during the period.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share, Diluted: $18.15. Indicates the effect of potential dilution from stock options and warrants.

Business Highlights

  • Business Focus Shift: In January 2024, Nukkleus Inc. ceased its general support service operations and shifted its focus to payment services. This strategic move involved terminating existing customer and supplier contracts with a related party.
  • Settlement Agreement: In November 2024, Nukkleus Inc. entered into a Settlement Agreement to sell its subsidiary that operates the payment services business to a shareholder for GBP 1,000. This transaction marks a significant shift in the company's operational focus.
  • Defense Sector Entry: In December 2024, Nukkleus Inc. signed a Securities Purchase Agreement with Star 26 Capital Inc. to acquire a controlling 51% interest in Star, an Israeli corporation engaged in supplying generators for 'iron dome' launchers and other defense products. This acquisition is set to pivot the company's business towards the defense sector.
  • Fiscal Year Change: Effective February 2025, the Board of Directors approved a change in the company's fiscal year end from September 30 to December 31, aligning financial reporting with the calendar year to enhance operational efficiency and comparability with industry peers.
  • Future Business Outlook: With the planned acquisition of Star, Nukkleus Inc. aims to focus its business operations on the defense sector, potentially expanding its market presence and operational capabilities in this industry.

SEC Filing: Nukkleus Inc. [ NUKK ] - 10-Q - May. 23, 2025
